item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me a lot of the premise of the Night's Dawn trilogy. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I recommend adding the pattern-screamer tag, since this anomaly is clearly related.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, whoops! I hadn't read this one when I posted SCP-2544, describing precisely the same phenomenon.

Your take on it was very different than mine, and it was cool to see! +1 :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't aware than the names were linked! Thanks for the heads up, I'll see if I can change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upon rereading, I've noticed something: Natasha is the informal form of the name Natalya, and an interviewer would use the latter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get it. It either reminds me too much of, or is too connected to SCP-2718 for me to upvote.

On the other hand...
Eh

I really appreciate the amount of effort you put into this. And the fact that this is your first SCP is all the more of a reason for me not to downvote it either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh dear, I see a DAMMERUNG.
This is amazingly scary and atmospheric, given the simplicity of concepts used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While this doesn't do anything particularly new, it manages to be more than the sum of it's parts. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cut out the final format screw at the end - I agree that it does feel kind of useless in context of the other existing addenda. Hopefully that makes it a little more focused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a lot I really like going on here - the concept and the execution of the format screw are both really working for me - but what's holding me back from an upvote is that it goes into too much detail. The direct letter from the ghosts is specifically what killed it for me - the changed text being technically just a restatement of what was already there is a really cool, original, cryptic way for the spirits to communicate; the direct threatening message was too on-the-nose, and I don't think it added nearly enough to make up for taking away the mystique the way it does. I felt similarly about the O5 note at the end. This was really evoking eerie, sentimental feelings that I think would be better served by leaving both of those out to be a little more mysterious (the other sections are already pretty clear about what's going on anyway, IMO).

This is still a fantastically well crafted article, and even the parts I didn't like are beautifully written - I think it would be a masterpiece without them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to like this; I really do. Unfortunately, I'm going to have to downvote.

First of all, this story shares a lot of similarities with SCP-3010, which I feel is executed better than this. Secondly, as you mentioned, there's a lot of moving parts here. From the format screws to the crosslinks to the story itself, I never really felt like these parts contributed to a good reading experience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HA

Oh you sad nihilists. You’re so funny.

Take my upvote, this is wonderfully composed. If I had to complain about anything, I’d say everything beyond the third addendum is kinda superfluous, but that’s just a minor nitpick.

*inhales* Ohh that was funny…

item.title: SCP-3675: The Void Called
item.content:encoded:
"We stand upon the brink of a precipice. We peer into the abyss—we grow sick and dizzy. Our first impulse is to shrink away from the danger. Unaccountably we remain… it is but a thought, although a fearful one, and one which chills the very marrow of our bones with the fierceness of the delight of its horror. It is merely the idea of what would be our sensations during the sweeping precipitancy of a fall from such a height… for this very cause do we now the most vividly desire it.”
—Edgar Allan Poe, "The Imp of the Perverse"

if your grave doesnt say "rest in peace" on it you are automatically drafted into the skeleton war
—wint (@dril)

Lurked three years, started a subreddit, and after more than eight months of work, my first SCP. There were many moving parts and plot elements to this that were difficult to get right, and virtually none of the words you read here are the same as my first draft of this. This piece is my take on a very personal sort of horror - one without any external threats or monsters, built purely out of what lies within humanity itself and nothing else.

Thanks to Photosynthetic, djkaktus, tretter, TheeSherm, BlazingTrail and Modern_Erasmus for feedback, and Minty Python on the SCPDeclassified Discord. Some CSS is taken from SCP-2136 and Wrong Proposal.
